title: Repeatedly applied bending strength stress-cycle factor Y_N
important_note: This JSON stores the equations explicitly shown in the figure. In the shaded high-cycle region, Shigley does not specify a single unique curve; the figure indicates the choice is influenced by pitchline velocity, gear material cleanliness, residual stress, and material ductility and fracture toughness.

low_to_mid_cycle_curves.approx_domain_note: These curves are shown from about 10^2 to the knee near a few times 10^6 cycles, with left-side plateaus as drawn.

400_HB.label: 400 HB
400_HB.notes: Upper hardness-specific curve shown in the figure.

250_HB.label: 250 HB
250_HB.notes: Intermediate hardness-specific curve shown in the figure.

160_HB.label: 160 HB
160_HB.notes: Lower hardness-specific curve shown in the figure.

high_cycle_shaded_region.approx_domain_note: Shaded region extends from the knee near about 3e6 cycles out to 1e10 cycles.

programmatic_use_note: Use the stored equations directly. If implementing a solver, apply the material-specific low/mid-cycle curve in its applicable range and use either the 'upper' or 'lower' shaded-region equation for high-cycle behavior according to engineering judgment or a documented selection rule.
